action->Vengeful Wrath.description: As long as it's not @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened}, the mitflit vermin cavalry gains a +2 status bonus to the DC of its Crawling Stabs ability against creatures that have previously damaged or tormented it.

Monk Cadre.name: Monk Cadre
Monk Cadre.publicNotes: Many martial artists train to defend themselves and their allies against unwanted aggression, but sometimes the circumstances dictate that even the most peaceful monks must go to war. A cadre of studied monks is capable of dishing out great damage with punches and kicks, as well as focusing their qi into ranged blasts.

action->Coordinated Maneuvers.name: Coordinated Maneuvers
action->Coordinated Maneuvers.description: The monk cadre is practiced at putting their foes off-balance. The monks choose [[/act disarm]], [[/act grapple]], [[/act reposition]], or [[/act reposition]] and attempt an Athletics check to perform that action, comparing the result to the appropriate DC (Fortitude for Grapple and Reposition, Reflex for Disarm and Trip) of each enemy within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5]. This can result in a different degree of success for each target.

action->Pummeling Punches.name: Pummeling Punches
action->Pummeling Punches.description:
1 to 3

Frequency once per round

Effect The monks perform well-timed coordinated melee attacks against all enemies in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5], with a @Check[reflex|dc:31|basic|options:area-effect] save. The damage depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[2d8[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage

2 @Damage[(4d8+8)[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage

3 @Damage[(4d8+16)[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage

action->Qi Blast.name: Qi Blast
action->Qi Blast.description: The monks channel their qi into an explosion of energy that affects all creatures in a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] within 60 feet. This explosion deals @Damage[6d6[force]|options:area-damage] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:31|basic|options:area-effect] save. When the monk cadre is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a @Template[type:burst|distance:5].

Ofalth Stampede.name: Ofalth Stampede
Ofalth Stampede.hpDetails: (4 segments), filth wallow; Thresholds 180 (3 segments), 90 (2 segments)
Ofalth Stampede.publicNotes: If a large city's waste management system spirals out of control, ofalths can grow and propagate unchecked. These reeking creatures haul tons of trash on their backs and carry a particularly virulent disease.

action->Refuse Pile.name: Refuse Pile
action->Refuse Pile.description: When an ofalth stampede is not in danger, they can spend 1 minute settling into a 20-foot pile that looks like a heap of garbage. Until the next time they take an action, the troop gains a +2 circumstance bonus to AC. A creature that enters the area of the garbage heap or interacts with it must attempt a save against the ofalth stampede's stench.

action->Filth Wallow.name: Filth Wallow
action->Filth Wallow.description: A trash stampede gains fast healing 10 when in an area with a high concentration of debris or excrement, such as a refuse heap or sewer.

action->Offal Rain.name: Offal Rain
action->Offal Rain.description: The ofalth stampede hurls a tremendous amount of rotting trash, which rains down in a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] within 60 feet. All creatures in the area take @Damage[4d10[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:33|basic|options:area-effect] save). Creatures that fail the saving throw are also exposed to wretched weeps. When the troop is reduced to 2 segments, the area decreases to a @Template[type:burst|distance:5].

action->Putrid Pummeling.name: Putrid Pummeling
action->Putrid Pummeling.description:
1 to 3

Frequency once per round

Effect The ofalths pummel all enemies in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10], with a @Check[reflex|dc:33|basic|options:area-effect] save. The damage depends on the number of actions. Creatures that fail the saving throw are exposed to wretched weeps.

1 @Damage[(1d12+3)[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage

2 @Damage[(3d12+7)[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage

3 @Damage[(4d12+10)[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage

action->Wretched Weeps.name: Wretched Weeps
action->Wretched Weeps.description:
Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:36]

Stage 1 carrier with no ill effect (1 day)

Stage 2 @Damage[2d4[bleed]]{2d4 persistent bleed} every hour and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} (1 day)

Stage 3 @Damage[2d6[bleed]]{2d6 persistent bleed} every hour and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 2} (1 day)

Omox Slime Pool.name: Omox Slime Pool
Omox Slime Pool.publicNotes: Omoxes are the embodiment of pollution and filth. While they have no true anatomy, they tend to take on mocking humanoid forms—though this distinction is difficult to spot when omoxes gather in large groups as part of an attacking force. Such a group often looks like a single roiling mass of slime with multiple torsos jutting up in a military formation.

spell->Translocate (At Will).name:
<Compendium> tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.VlNcjmYyu95vOUe8]
Translocate (At Will)

spell->Create Water (At Will).name:
<Compendium> tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.WzLKjSw6hsBhuklC]
Create Water (At Will)

action->Clean Vulnerability.name: Clean Vulnerability
action->Clean Vulnerability.description: Omoxes embody filth, and they find the concept of cleanliness abhorrent. An omox slime pool subjected to an effect that cleans them takes @Damage[4d6[mental]] damage. They also take this damage the first time each round a creature damaged by an omox slime pool spends actions cleaning off the resultant filth.

action->+1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic.name: +1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic

action->Absorb Weapon.name: Absorb Weapon
action->Absorb Weapon.description:
Trigger A creature hits the omox slime pool with a melee weapon

Effect The omoxes attempt to [[/act disarm]] the creature. On a critical success, the weapon becomes subsumed within the body of an omox rather than falling to the ground. Retrieving the weapon requires a successful [[/act disarm dc=45]]{DC 45 Athletics} check to Disarm.

action->Slime Barrage.name: Slime Barrage
action->Slime Barrage.description: The omoxes hurl balls of heavy slime in a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] within 30 feet. All creatures in the area take @Damage[4d6[bludgeoning],2d6[acid]|options:area-damage]{4d6 bludgeoning damage and 2d6 acid damage} (@Check[reflex|dc:35|basic|options:area-effect] save). A creature that fails the save is mired in the slime, taking a –10-foot circumstance penalty to its Speeds for 1 minute or until it [[/act escape dc=38]]{Escapes}; on a critical failure, the creature is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} for the same duration. When the slime pool is reduced to 2 segments, the area decreases to a @Template[type:burst|distance:5].

action->Smothering Grasp.name: Smothering Grasp
action->Smothering Grasp.description:
Requirements The omox slime pool has a creature @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.VcDeM8A5oI6VqhbM]{Restrained}

Effect Omox slime flows onto the creature, completely covering it. The creature must then succeed at a @Check[fortitude|dc:38] save or it becomes @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} and must hold its breath or begin @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.journals.JournalEntry.S55aqwWIzpQRFhcq.JournalEntryPage.OiRwxbfLNUniLg15#drowning-and-suffocating]{suffocating}. These effects last as long as the omoxes have the creature grabbed or restrained.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.other-effects.Item.2u26bmArDPuoCljc]{Effect: Remaining Air}

action->Waves of Sludge.name: Waves of Sludge
action->Waves of Sludge.description:
1 to 3

Frequency once per round

Effect The omoxes attack all enemies in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] with slimy tendrils (@Check[reflex|dc:35|basic|options:area-effect] save). A creature that critically fails this saving throw is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.kWc1fhmv9LBiTuei]{Grabbed} by the slime pool. The damage depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[(1d6+3)[bludgeoning],1d6[acid]|options:area-damage]{1d6+3 bludgeoning damage plus 1d6 acid damage}

2 @Damage[(3d6+12)[bludgeoning],2d6[acid]|options:area-damage]{3d6+12 bludgeoning damage plus 2d6 acid damage}

3 @Damage[(4d6+14)[bludgeoning],3d6[acid]|options:area-damage]{4d6+14 bludgeoning damage plus 3d6 acid damage}

Orc Raiding Party.name: Orc Raiding Party
Orc Raiding Party.publicNotes: Orc raiding parties are feared units whose unrelenting attacks and sheer ferocity make them capable, if not subtle, soldiers. Orc raiders take the duty of carrying their hold's banner into combat with deadly seriousness and will gladly risk grievous injuries to keep their hold's standard flying high.

action->Ferocious Fall.name: Ferocious Fall
action->Ferocious Fall.description:
The orc raiding party is about to lose a segment due to passing a Hit Point threshold

Effect The dying orc raiders lash out as they fall. Each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] takes @Damage[(1d6+2)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:19|basic|options:area-effect] save); this occurs before the raiding party loses a segment.

action->Break Through.name: Break Through
action->Break Through.description:
Frequency once per 10 minutes

Effect The orc raiders exploit a gap in enemy lines. The orc raiding party Strides twice; it can pass through spaces of Medium or smaller creatures but can't end its movement in them. All enemies whose spaces the orc raiding party passed through or were adjacent to at any point during their movement take @Damage[(1d6+2)[piercing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:19|basic|options:area-effect,forced-movement] save). A creature who critically fails this save is also pushed 5 feet away from the orc raiding party. Break Through damages each creature only once.

action->Iron Rain.name: Iron Rain
action->Iron Rain.description: The orc raiders launch a multitude of javelins at foes in a deadly volley. This volley is a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] within 30 feet that deals @Damage[3d6[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage with a @Check[reflex|dc:19|basic|options:area-effect] save. When the orc raiding party is reduced to 2 or fewer segments, this area decreases to a @Template[type:burst|distance:5].

action->Rip Them Up.name: Rip Them Up
action->Rip Them Up.description:
1 to 3

Frequency once per round

Effect The orc raiders batter all enemies in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] with coordinated knuckle dagger strikes (@Check[reflex|dc:19|basic|options:area-effect] save). The damage depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[(1d6+2)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage

2 @Damage[(2d6+5)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage

3 @Damage[(3d6+7)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage

Protean Tumult.name: Protean Tumult
Protean Tumult.publicNotes: Due to the inherently chaotic nature of proteans, many would assume they are incapable of cooperation. However, they are cunning enough to be able to unite in the face of danger or at the behest of more powerful beings, sometimes acting as a group called a protean tumult. Such an association is comprising of dozens of proteans of different shapes and sizes, many of whom continually alter their forms from moment to moment.
Protean Tumult.saveDetails: +1 status to all saves vs. magic

spell->Unfettered Movement (Constant).name:
<Compendium> tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.aqRYNoSvxsVfqglH]
Unfettered Movement (Constant)

effect->Spell Effect: Unfettered Movemen.name: Spell Effect: Unfettered Movement (Constant)
effect->Spell Effect: Unfettered Movemen.description:
Granted by @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.aqRYNoSvxsVfqglH]{Unfettered Movement}

You ignore circumstance penalties to your Speed.

action->Entropy Sense.name: Entropy Sense
action->Entropy Sense.description: A protean tumult can anticipate the most likely presence of a creature through a supernatural insight into chaotic probabilities and chance. This grants it the ability to sense creatures within the listed range. @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.EoKBlgf6Smt8opaU]{Veil of Privacy} prevents a creature from being detected via entropy sense automatically (without a counteract check).

action->+1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic.name: +1 Status to All Saves vs. Magic

action->Protean Anatomy 12.name: Protean Anatomy 12
action->Protean Anatomy 12.description:
A protean's vital organs shift and change shape and position constantly. Immediately after the protean takes acid, electricity, or sonic damage, it gains the listed amount of resistance to that damage type. This lasts for 1 hour or until the next time the protean takes damage of one of the other types (in which case its resistance changes to match that type), whichever comes first. The protean is immune to polymorph effects unless it is a willing target. If @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.XgEqL1kFApUbl5Z2]{Blinded} or @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.9PR9y0bi4JPKnHPR]{Deafened}, the protean automatically recovers at the end of its next turn as new sensory organs grow to replace the compromised ones.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.c6SiBB3mzV8lZUQr]{Effect: Protean Anatomy}

action->Chaos Flux.name: Chaos Flux
action->Chaos Flux.description: A protean tumult is less organized and more vicious than most troops. It can move into other creatures' spaces, and other creatures can move into its spaces. Its spaces are difficult terrain to non-protean creatures. A creature that willingly moves into a protean tumult's space takes @Damage[(1d12+1)[untyped]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:29|basic|options:area-effect] save); a creature takes this damage only once per round.

action->Chaos Strike.name: Chaos Strike
action->Chaos Strike.description:
Frequency once per round

Effect The protean tumult chooses adamantine, cold iron, or silver; the damage dealt by its Claws, Jaws, and Tails is treated as that material for 1 minute or until it uses Chaos Strike again.

action->Claws, Jaws, and Tails.name: Claws, Jaws, and Tails

1 to 3

1 @Damage[(1d12+1)[untyped]|options:area-damage] damage

2 @Damage[(2d12+10)[untyped]|options:area-damage] damage

3 @Damage[(3d12+11)[untyped]|options:area-damage] damage

action->Stupefying Swipe.name: Stupefying Swipe
action->Stupefying Swipe.description: The protean tumult makes their way across the battlefield. It Strides. At the end of this movement, they lash out at the enemy with tentacles and other blunt body parts, dealing @Damage[(2d12+10)[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] (@Check[reflex|dc:29|basic|options:area-effect,inflicts:stupefied] save). A creature who fails this save is also @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} for 2 rounds (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 3} on a critical failure).

Qadiran Camel Corps.name: Qadiran Camel Corps
Qadiran Camel Corps.publicNotes: Navigating the deserts of Golarion requires trained individuals and often specialized mounts to keep them safe. The Qadiran camel corps are one such example of those who embark on long patrols, hunt down brigands, and dispatch any natural threats that might trouble travelers.

action->Desert-Adapted Troop.name: Desert-Adapted Troop
action->Desert-Adapted Troop.description: A camel corps is well-adapted to heat and deserts. They treat environmental heat as if it was one step less severe, and if the camel mounts have eaten and drank their fill, the corps can Subsist for 1 week without needing to attempt Survival checks.

action->Dust Storm.name: Dust Storm
action->Dust Storm.description:
Requirements The camel corps is in a desert or similar region

Effect The camel corps Steps, whipping up sand into a short-lived storm that obscures the surrounding region. Until the end of their next turn, the camel corps is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} from all creatures more than 15 feet away, and all creatures more than 15 feet away are concealed to the camel corps.

action->Mounted Troop.name: Mounted Troop
action->Mounted Troop.description: Effects that target only animals or only humanoids may not work on the valkyrie tempest, subject to the GM's discretion.

action->Reflective Arrows.name: Reflective Arrows
action->Reflective Arrows.description:
Adjusting to the angle of the sun, the camel riders draw their shortbows, then launch a ranged attack in the form of an arcing volley. This volley is a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] within 60 feet that deals @Damage[3d6[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic|options:area-effect] save). In areas of bright light, such as outside during the day, the targets take a –1 circumstance penalty to the save. When the camel corps is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a @Template[type:burst|distance:5].

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.owOGKBjP7tlyQD5o]{Effect: Reflective Arrows (Penalty)}

action->Scimitar Assault.name: Scimitar Assault
action->Scimitar Assault.description:
1 to 3

Frequency once per round

Effect The camel corps engages in a coordinated melee attack against each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic|options:area-effect] save). The damage depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[(1d6+1)[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage

2 @Damage[(2d6+7)[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage

3 @Damage[(2d6+11)[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage

action->Trailblazing Stride.name: Trailblazing Stride
action->Trailblazing Stride.description: While moving on land, the Qadiran camel corps ignores the effects of non-magical difficult terrain.

action->Dirty Tricks.name: Dirty Tricks
action->Dirty Tricks.description: The ratfolk feint and trip up their foes in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5], attempting a single @Check[thievery|against:reflex] check and comparing the result to each target's Reflex DC. On a success, the target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2} on a critical success) until they take an Interact action to remove the condition.

action->Poisoned Bolts.name: Poisoned Bolts
action->Poisoned Bolts.description: The shank squad draws hand crossbows to fire poisoned bolts in a volley. This volley is a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] within 60 feet that deals @Damage[2d6[piercing],2d6[poison]|options:area-damage]{2d6 piercing damage and 2d6 poison damage} with a @Check[reflex|dc:22|basic|options:area-effect] save. When the shank squad is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a @Template[type:burst|distance:5].

action->Shank 'Em.name: Shank 'Em
action->Shank 'Em.description:
1 to 3

Frequency once per round

Effect The shank squad makes a coordinated attack with shivs and daggers against each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5], with a @Check[reflex|dc:22|basic|options:area-effect] save. The damage depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[1d6[slashing],1d4[precision]|options:area-damage]{1d6 slashing damage and 1d4 precision damage}

2 @Damage[(2d6+3)[slashing],2d4[precision]|options:area-damage]{2d6+3 slashing damage and 2d4 precision damage}

3 @Damage[(2d6+6)[slashing],3d4[precision]|options:area-damage]{2d6+6 slashing damage and 3d4 precision damage}

Redcap Brigade.name: Redcap Brigade
Redcap Brigade.publicNotes: Redcaps are sadistic fey known for their merciless bloodlust. When they gather into troops, they goad each other to greater heights of violence.

action->Divine Revulsion.name: Divine Revulsion
action->Divine Revulsion.description:
If a redcap brigade sees a creature brandish a religious symbol of a deity (which requires an Interact action by that creature) or cast a divine spell while wearing a religious symbol, the troop must attempt a @Check[will|dc:26] save. They then become temporarily immune to all brandished religious symbols for 10 minutes.

Critical Success The troop is unaffected.

Success The troop is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 1}.

Failure The troop is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}.

Critical Failure The troop is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 3}.

action->Blood Soak.name: Blood Soak
action->Blood Soak.description:
Trigger The redcap brigade loses its first segment, causes another troop to lose its first segment, or is otherwise exposed to copious amounts of blood

Effect The redcap brigade gains a status bonus to damage rolls for 1 minute. The bonus is +2 if they spend one action on Bloody Reaping, +6 if they spend two actions, or +8 if they spend 3 actions. They gain a +4 status bonus to damage rolls with Bowl Over and Stomp.

action->Bloody Reaping.name: Bloody Reaping
action->Bloody Reaping.description:
1 to 3

Frequency once per round

Effect The redcaps in the brigade wildly swing their halberds and sickles at each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:10], with a @Check[reflex|dc:26|basic|options:area-effect] save. The damage depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[(1d10+2)[slashing]|options:area-damage,bloody-reaping-one]

2 @Damage[(2d10+9)[slashing]|options:area-damage,bloody-reaping-two]

3 @Damage[(3d10+10)[slashing]|options:area-damage,bloody-reaping-three]

action->Bowl Over and Stomp.name: Bowl Over and Stomp
action->Bowl Over and Stomp.description:
The redcap brigade Strides; they can pass through spaces of Medium or smaller creatures, but can't end their movement in them. All enemies whose spaces the redcap brigade passed through take @Damage[4d8[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage,bowl-ower-and-stomp] damage and must attempt a @Check[fortitude|dc:26|options:area-effect,inflicts:prone] save. Bowl Over and Stomp damages each creature only once.

Critical Success The creature takes no damage.

Success The creature takes half damage. If it is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone}, it also takes @Damage[1d6[persistent,bleed]] damage.

Failure The creature takes full damage, is knocked prone, and takes @Damage[2d6[persistent,bleed]] damage.

Critical Failure The creature takes double damage, is knocked prone, and takes @Damage[2d6[persistent,bleed]] damage.

action->Deadly Swipes.name: Deadly Swipes
action->Deadly Swipes.description:
Trigger The redcap brigade drops a creature to 0 Hit Points with Bloody Reaping

Effect The redcap brigade performs an additional one-action Bloody Reaping, ignoring the once per round frequency limitation. This does not deal damage to the triggering creature.

Scamp Avalanche.name: Scamp Avalanche
Scamp Avalanche.publicNotes: In general, all elemental scamps are a bit mischievous, and equally as likely to gather in masses to wreak (often accidental) havoc.

action->Scree Breath.name: Scree Breath
action->Scree Breath.description: The scamp avalanche breathes rocks in a @Template[type:cone|distance:15] that deals @Damage[3d6[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage to each creature within the area (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic|options:area-effect] save). The scamp avalanche can't use Scree Breath again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Scree Breath]]{1d4 rounds}.

Scamp Inferno.name: Scamp Inferno
Scamp Inferno.publicNotes: Fire scamps are playful and excitable pranksters from the Plane of Fire that have little concept of moderation. When they gather into flocks, unintentional arson is the inevitable result.

action->Smoke Vision.name: Smoke Vision
action->Smoke Vision.description: The scamp inferno ignores the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} condition from smoke.

action->Burning Bites.name: Burning Bites
action->Burning Bites.description:
1 to 3

The scamp inferno attacks with a flurry of tiny teeth and flickering flames. Each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:21|basic|options:area-effect] save. The damage depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[1d6[piercing],1d4[fire]|options:area-damage]{1d6 piercing damage and 1d4 fire damage}

2 @Damage[(2d6+2)[piercing],2d4[fire]|options:area-damage]{2d6+2 piercing damage and 2d4 fire damage}

3 @Damage[(2d6+4)[piercing],3d4[fire]|options:area-damage]{2d6+4 piercing damage and 3d4 fire damage}

action->Flame Breath.name: Flame Breath
action->Flame Breath.description: The scamp inferno breathes flames in a @Template[type:cone|distance:15] that deals @Damage[3d6[fire]|options:area-damage] damage to each creature within the area (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic|options:area-effect] save). Creatures that fail the save also take @Damage[1d6[persistent,fire]|options:area-damage] damage. The scamp inferno can't use Flame Breath again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Flame Breath]]{1d4 rounds}.

1 to 3

Creatures that fail the save also take @Damage[1d6[bleed]] damage.The scamp shrapnel can't use Shrapnel Breath again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Shrapnel Breath]]{1d4 rounds}.

action->Pollen Breath.description: The scamp tangle breathes pollen in a @Template[type:cone|distance:15] that deals @Damage[3d6[poison]|options:area-damage] damage to each creature within the area (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic|options:area-effect] save). The scamp tangle can't use Pollen Breath again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Pollen Breath]]{1d4 rounds}.

Scamp Whirlwind.name: Scamp Whirlwind
Scamp Whirlwind.publicNotes: In general, all elemental scamps are a bit mischievous, and equally as likely to gather in masses to wreak (often accidental) havoc.

action->Fog Vision.name: Fog Vision
action->Fog Vision.description: The scamp whirlwind ignores the @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.DmAIPqOBomZ7H95W]{Concealed} condition from fog.

action->Sirocco Breath.name: Sirocco Breath
action->Sirocco Breath.description: The scamp whirlwind breathes in a @Template[type:cone|distance:15] that deals @Damage[3d6[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage to each creature within the area (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic|options:area-effect,forced-movement] save). Creatures that fail the save also get pushed back by 10 feet. The scamp whirlwind can't use Sirocco Breath again for [[/gmr 1d4 #Recharge Sirocco Breath]]{1d4 rounds}.

action->Sonic Slams.name: Sonic Slams
action->Sonic Slams.description:
1 to 3

The scamp whirlwind attacks with a flurry of tiny slams. Each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:21|basic|options:area-effect] save. The damage depends on the number of actions.

2 @Damage[(2d6+2)[bludgeoning],2d4[sonic]|options:area-damage]{2d6+2 bludgeoning damage and 2d4 sonic damage}

Sinswarm.name: Sinswarm
Sinswarm.publicNotes: Sinspawn, monstrosities of twisted humanoid flesh, were created ages ago by the runelord Alaznist to serve as shock troops. Hateful even toward their own kind, sinspawn usually band together only in small, isolated cults, although powerful beings are occasionally able to coerce greater numbers of these slavering horrors to join forces as uniquely bloodthirsty and destructive mobs.

action->Sin Scent.name: Sin Scent
action->Sin Scent.description: A sinswarm can smell creatures reflecting any one of the seven primary sins as the scent ability. The GM determines which creatures are appropriately sinful.

action->Reactive Attack.name: Reactive Attack
action->Reactive Attack.description:
Trigger A creature within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] of the sinswarm uses a manipulate action or a move action, makes a ranged attack, or leaves a square during a move action it's using

Effect The sinswarm lashes out at the triggering creature, dealing @Damage[(2d8+9)[piercing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic|options:area-effect] save). If the creature critically fails the save and the trigger was a manipulate action, that action is disrupted.

action->Sinful Assault.name: Sinful Assault
action->Sinful Assault.description:
1 to 3

Frequency once per round

Effect The sinswarm makes a coordinated attack against each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5], with a @Check[reflex|dc:25|basic|options:area-effect] save. The damage depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[(1d8+2)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage

2 @Damage[(2d8+9)[piercing]|options:area-damage,sinful-bite] damage plus sinful bite

3 @Damage[(3d8+11)[piercing]|options:area-damage,sinful-bite] damage plus sinful bite

action->Sinful Bite.name: Sinful Bite
action->Sinful Bite.description:
A creature bitten by a sinspawn must attempt a @Check[will|dc:28] save as it is assailed by sinful thoughts. The sinswarm can't inflict the same sin effect on multiple targets in the same round until it has inflicted all seven sins at least once.

Critical Success The creature is unaffected.

Success The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 1}.

Failure The creature is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.fesd1n5eVhpCSS18]{Sickened 2}.

Critical Failure The creature is sickened 2 and takes one of the following additional effects, chosen by the GM:

  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 2} for 1 minute (envy)

  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1} (gluttony)

  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2} for 1 minute (greed)

  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2} for 1 minute (lust)

  • @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} and @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.MIRkyAjyBeXivMa7]{Enfeebled 1} for 1 minute (pride)

  • –10-foot status penalty to all Speeds for 1 minute (sloth) @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.9Qyu0HN5j6DO8Izc]{Effect: Sinful Bite}

  • drained 1 and enfeebled 1 for 1 minute (wrath)

Skeleton Mob.name: Skeleton Mob
Skeleton Mob.publicNotes: To the morbid, a battlefield is no more than a garden of corpses. Sometimes, either through the magic of a necromancer or through an unfortunate pooling of void energy, these bodies rise up as broken and battered skeletons. While some of these skeletons are far from whole, they can still pose a significant threat.

action->Ossuary Storm.name: Ossuary Storm
action->Ossuary Storm.description: The skeleton mob hurls skulls and fragments of bone in a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] within 30 feet. This attack deals @Damage[3d6[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic|options:area-effect] save). When the skeleton mob is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a @Template[type:burst|distance:5].

action->Rattling Bones.name: Rattling Bones
action->Rattling Bones.description:
1 to 3

Frequency once per round

Effect The skeletons engage using their claws and broken bones to attack each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5], with a @Check[reflex|dc:21|basic|options:area-effect] save. The damage depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[(1d4+1)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage

2 @Damage[(2d4+7)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage

3 @Damage[(3d4+10)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage

Vanth Guardian Flock.name: Vanth Guardian Flock
Vanth Guardian Flock.publicNotes: Vanth psychopomps are eternal guardians of the cycle of life and death. When souls are threatened by fiends or other malevolent forces, they gather together to cut the threat down.

spell->Translocate (At Will).name:
<Compendium> tag will get replaced with text from compendium entry @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.VlNcjmYyu95vOUe8]

action->Reactive Relocation.name: Reactive Relocation
action->Reactive Relocation.description:
Trigger A creature hits the guardian flock with an attack roll

Effect After the attack roll is resolved, the troop pools dimensional magic to rapidly change their position. They cast 4th-rank @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.spells-srd.Item.VlNcjmYyu95vOUe8]{Translocate}, except their range is limited to 40 feet.

action->Guardians' Curse.name: Guardians' Curse
action->Guardians' Curse.description:
Frequency three times per day

Effect The guardian flock bestows a curse upon all enemies in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] by touching them with their scythes. Each affected creature must attempt a @Check[will|dc:33|options:area-effect] save.

Critical Success The target is unaffected and is temporarily immune to Guardians' Curse for 24 hours.

Success The target feels a momentary shudder of doom and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 1} for 1 minute by the distracting sensation.

Failure The target becomes morose and glum as it accepts its own inevitable fate. For 1 hour, the target is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 2}. Each time the target gains the dying condition, the stupefied condition value increases by 1, to a maximum value of @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.e1XGnhKNSQIm5IXg]{Stupefied 4}.

Critical Failure As failure, but the effect is permanent.

action->Harvest the Wicked.name: Harvest the Wicked
action->Harvest the Wicked.description:
1 to 3

Frequency once per round

Effect The vanths swing their scythes in a coordinated melee attack. Each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:30|basic|options:area-effect] save. The damage depends on the number of actions. The slashing damage is treated as adamantine, cold iron, and silver.

1 @Damage[(1d10+1)[slashing],1d6[untyped]|options:area-damage,item:rune:property:ghost-touch]{1d10+1 slashing damage plus 1d6 shepherd's touch}

2 @Damage[(2d10+3)[slashing],3d6[untyped]|options:area-damage,item:rune:property:ghost-touch]{2d10+3 slashing damage plus 3d6 shepherd's touch}

3 @Damage[(2d10+7)[slashing],4d6[untyped]|options:area-damage,item:rune:property:ghost-touch]{2d10+7 slashing damage plus 4d6 shepherd's touch}

action->Shepherd's Touch.name: Shepherd's Touch
action->Shepherd's Touch.description: The physical damage dealt by the guardian flock's Harvest the Wicked ability is treated as coming from a weapon with a ghost touch property rune. In addition, the vanths deal the listed damage as void damage to living creatures or vitality damage to undead.

Viking Guard.name: Viking Guard
Viking Guard.publicNotes: Battle-tested Ulfen barbarians, hunters, and warriors gather into an elite fighting unit who specialize in offering protection to their leaders in battle as well as protecting those who can afford their services. Traveling far and wide, single troops or entire companies can easily find work based on their reputations, from serving as bodyguards in small private armies to taking on specialized jobs.

action->+2 Status to All Saves vs. Fear.name: +2 Status to All Saves vs. Fear

action->Sacrifice.name: Sacrifice
action->Sacrifice.description:
Requirements The viking guard has a charge, and that creature is adjacent to the viking guard

Trigger The viking guard's charge takes Hit Point damage

Effect The viking guard's charge takes half damage, and the viking guard takes the remainder of the damage.

action->Berserker Strikes.name: Berserker Strikes
action->Berserker Strikes.description:
1 to 3

Frequency once per round

Effect Battle axes in hand, the viking guard engages in a coordinated melee attack against enemies in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5], with a @Check[reflex|dc:27|basic|options:area-effect] save. The damage depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[(1d8+3)[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage

2 @Damage[(2d8+12)[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage

3 @Damage[(3d8+15)[slashing]|options:area-damage] damage

action->Guard Charge.name: Guard Charge
action->Guard Charge.description:
The viking guard designates an ally it can see to be its charge. The charge gains a +2 circumstance bonus to their AC, Reflex saves, and saves against fear when they are adjacent to the viking guard. Further, this allows the viking guard to use its Sacrifice and Shield Wall actions. A viking guard can have only one charge at a time, and if it designates a new charge, the old one loses all benefits. If the viking guard's charge is reduced to 0 Hit Points, the viking guard must succeed at a @Check[will|dc:30] save or become @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.TBSHQspnbcqxsmjL]{Frightened 2}; this is an emotion, fear, and mental effect.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.qTbVcHLfFDuyJTTG]{Effect: Guard Charge}

action->Shield Wall.name: Shield Wall
action->Shield Wall.description:
Requirements The viking guard has a charge, and that creature is adjacent to the viking guard

Effect Raising shields, the viking guard Strides up to twice its Speed as it protects its charge. The viking guard gains a +2 circumstance bonus to its AC against reactions triggered by this movement. The viking guard's charge can Stride the same distance as a reaction, moving with the troop to maintain the bonuses from Guard Charge during this movement.

Vordine Legion.name: Vordine Legion
Vordine Legion.publicNotes: Vordines are the foot soldiers of Hell, dispatched in vast armies from the iron city of Dis to guard Hell's uppermost layers and crush the enemies of the archdevils who rule there. Indefatigable and pitiless, a legion of vordines strikes with a ruthless precision that commands fear and envy throughout the multiverse in equal measure. Although they usually serve at the command of more powerful devils, such as the tyrannical nessari, every legion also observes a unique internal hierarchy that enables another vordine to assume command at a moment's notice should it become necessary.
Vordine Legion.saveDetails: +1 status to all saves vs. magic

spell->Translocate (At Will).name:

Translocate (At Will)

action->Reactive Attack.name: Reactive Attack
action->Reactive Attack.description:
Trigger A creature within the vordine legion's reach uses a manipulate action or a move action, makes a ranged attack, or leaves a square during a move action it's using

Effect The creature takes @Damage[(2d8+11)[piercing]|options:area-damage|traits:magical,unholy] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:26|basic|options:area-effect] save); this damage has the magical and unholy traits. If the creature critically fails its saving throw and the trigger was a manipulate action, the legion disrupts that action.

action->Burning March.name: Burning March
action->Burning March.description: The vordine legion Strides, leaving an orderly pattern of burning hoofprints in each square they enter. The hoofprints continue to burn for 1 minute. Any creature on the ground that begins its turn in, or enters a square with, burning hoofprints takes @Damage[2d8[fire]] damage.

action->Impaling Barrage.name: Impaling Barrage
action->Impaling Barrage.description: The vordine legion releases a hail of tridents. This hail is a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] within 40 feet that deals @Damage[4d8[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:26|basic|options:area-effect,inflicts:clumsy] save). Creatures that fail the saving throw are @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 1} until the start of the vordine legion's next turn (@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2} on a critical failure). When the vordines are reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a @Template[type:burst|distance:5].

action->Tines of Dis.name: Tines of Dis
action->Tines of Dis.description:
1 to 3

Frequency once per round

Effect The vordines of the legion make coordinated melee attacks with their tridents. Each enemy within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] must attempt a @Check[reflex|dc:26|basic|options:area-effect] save. The damage depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[(1d8+2)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage

2 @Damage[(2d8+11)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage

3 @Damage[(3d8+14)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage

Wight Battalion.name: Wight Battalion
Wight Battalion.publicNotes: Wights are malevolent undead, bent on desecrating the holy and determined to cause suffering to all living creatures. A lone wight is dangerous enough, but when compelled to act together, they are a force to be reckoned with. A wight battalion has the potential to curse entire armies, increasing their numbers until they become an unholy tide.

action->Final Grudge.name: Final Grudge
action->Final Grudge.description:
Trigger The wight battalion is about to lose a segment due to Hit Point damage

Effect The wights strike out as they fall. Each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] takes @Damage[2d4[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:25|basic|options:area-effect] save). This occurs before the battalion loses a segment.

action->Fueled by Spite.name: Fueled by Spite
action->Fueled by Spite.description:
Each time a creature loses Hit Points due to the wight battalion's corrupting spite curse, the battalion gains 6 temporary Hit Points that last for 1 round.

@UUID[Compendium.pf2e.bestiary-effects.Item.yeK8zmBJvam75Fp7]{Effect: Fueled by Spite}

action->Corrupting Spite.name: Corrupting Spite
action->Corrupting Spite.description:
The wight battalion's attacks inflict a curse that makes a creature grow weak and spiteful. A living humanoid that dies while under this curse rises as a @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.DBTbqI9QQRtlJwWh]{Wight} after [[/gmr 1d4 #rounds]]{1d4 rounds}, controlled by the wight battalion that inflicted the curse. This new wight can't inflict corrupting spite and is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2}. If the creating wight battalion dies or after roughly a month of existence, the new wight becomes autonomous and becomes a normal wight

Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:25]

Stage 1 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1} (1 round)

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 2} and doesn't treat any creatures as allies (1 round)

Stage 3 As stage 2, except @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 3} (1 round)

Stage 4 As stage 2, except @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 4} (1 round).

action->Hateful Daggers.name: Hateful Daggers
action->Hateful Daggers.description:
1 to 3

The wights coordinate melee attacks with the daggers they were buried with. Each enemy within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] attempts a @Check[reflex|dc:25|basic|options:area-effect] save. The damage depends on the number of actions. On a failed save, the creature is also exposed to corrupting spite.

1 @Damage[2d4[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage

2 @Damage[(4d4+8)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage

Wolf Pack.name: Wolf Pack
Wolf Pack.publicNotes: Though wolf packs can be a menace to farming communities that rely on livestock, they prefer not to go after humanoids or other dangerous foes. However, hungry packs don't have the luxury of being cautious.

action->Harry Prey.name: Harry Prey
action->Harry Prey.description: The wolf pack focuses all their efforts on biting a single adjacent foe, who takes @Damage[(4d6+8)[piercing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic] save). If the creature fails this saving throw, the wolf pack can immediately attempt an [[/act trip]]{Athletics} check to @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.actionspf2e.Item.ge56Lu1xXVFYUnLP]{Trip} the creature.

action->Pack Hunt.name: Pack Hunt
action->Pack Hunt.description:
1 to 3

The wolves work together to fell their opponents. Each enemy within a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] attempts a @Check[reflex|dc:21|basic|options:area-effect] save. A creature who is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.j91X7x0XSomq8d60]{Prone} is @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.i3OJZU2nk64Df3xm]{Clumsy 2} for this attack. The damage depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[(1d6+1)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage

2 @Damage[(2d6+7)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage

3 @Damage[(2d6+11)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage

Xulgath Army.name: Xulgath Army
Xulgath Army.publicNotes: Though most xulgath settlements can be found in the Darklands, these reptilian humanoids sometimes venture to the surface with ill intent. When gathered into an army, xulgaths tear through innocent communities in the name of whatever leader they serve.

action->Club Offensive.description:
1 to 3

Frequency once per round

Effect The xulgaths make coordinated melee attacks against each enemy in @Template[type:emanation|distance:5], with a @Check[reflex|dc:21|basic|options:area-effect] save. The damage dealt depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[(1d6+1)[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage

2 @Damage[(2d6+7)[bludgeoning]|options:area-damage] damage

action->Javelin Barrage.name: Javelin Barrage
action->Javelin Barrage.description: he xulgaths draw javelins and launch a coordinated barrage at range. This barrage is a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] within 30 feet that deals @Damage[3d6[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic|options:area-effect] save). When the xulgath army is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a @Template[type:burst|distance:5].

action->Rend Flesh.name: Rend Flesh
action->Rend Flesh.description: The xulgaths concentrate their attacks on a single adjacent enemy, clawing and biting with abandon. That creature takes @Damage[(3d4+5)[slashing]] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:21|basic] save). On a failed save, the creature also takes @Damage[1d4[persistent,bleed]] damage.

Xulgath Dinosaur Cavalry.name: Xulgath Dinosaur Cavalry
Xulgath Dinosaur Cavalry.publicNotes: Xulgaths have been breeding and training dinosaurs for millennia, and the bonds between them are as familiar and strong as that between any other humanoid and their domesticated companion of choice. While most of the creatures are used primarily as beasts of burden, elite xulgath soldiers known as roughriders train the most vicious and aggressive dinosaurs from numerous species to serve as vicious and terrifying mounts, capable of punching through defensive lines and smashing fortifications to splinters as easily as they scatter enemy soldiers.

action->Javelin Barrage.name: Javelin Barrage
action->Javelin Barrage.description: The xulgaths draw javelins and launch a coordinated barrage at range. This barrage is a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] within 30 feet that deals @Damage[4d8[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:30|basic|options:area-effect] save). When the cavalry is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a @Template[type:burst|distance:5].

action->Mounted Troop.name: Mounted Troop
action->Mounted Troop.description: Effects that target only animals or only humanoids may not work on the valkyrie tempest, subject to the GM's discretion.

action->Spears, Teeth, and Horns.description:
1 to 3

Frequency once per round

Effect The cavalry makes a coordinated melee attack against each enemy in @Template[type:emanation|distance:10], with a @Check[reflex|dc:30|basic|options:area-effect] save. The damage dealt depends on the number of actions.

Zecui Horde.name: Zecui Horde
Zecui Horde.publicNotes: A surprise raid by a swarm of the insectile zecui can easily wipe out an entire village overnight. When they don't devour their victims immediately, zecuis typically implant them with their own larvae and bury them in sprawling mass graves, where the developing zecui horde can lie dormant for years before suddenly erupting to the surface to wreak havoc on nearby settlements.

action->Harden Chitin.name: Harden Chitin
action->Harden Chitin.description: The zecuis fuse their chitin into black metallic shells. The horde gains resistance 10 to all damage (except mental and spirit) until they next take a move action.

action->Mandible Frenzy.name: Mandible Frenzy
action->Mandible Frenzy.description:
1 to 3

Frequency once per round

Effect The horde makes a vicious bite attack against each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] (@Check[reflex|dc:27|basic|options:area-effect] save). The damage dealt depends on the number of actions.

1 @Damage[(1d8+2)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage

2 @Damage[(2d8+12)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage

3 @Damage[(3d8+15)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage

action->Mucus Deluge.name: Mucus Deluge
action->Mucus Deluge.description: The horde spits a volley of larva-infested mucus as a @Template[type:burst|distance:10] within 30 feet. Each creature in the area must succeed at a @Check[reflex|dc:27|options:area-effect,inflicts:immobilized] save or be stuck to the nearest surface, @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.eIcWbB5o3pP6OIMe]{Immobilized} until they [[/act escape dc=30]]. Any creature so immobilized is exposed to zecui larvae at the end of each of its turns. When the zecui horde is reduced to 2 segments, this area decreases to a @Template[type:burst|distance:5].

action->Subterranean Ambush.name: Subterranean Ambush
action->Subterranean Ambush.description:
Requirements The zecui horde has burrowed into an ambush position just beneath a surface of dirt, sand, or a similar loose material

Effect The horde bursts from the ground and moves up to its Speed. The horde deals @Damage[(1d8+2)[piercing]|options:area-damage] damage (@Check[reflex|dc:29|basic|options:area-effect] save) to each enemy in a @Template[type:emanation|distance:5] at the end of this movement.

action->Zecui Larvae.name: Zecui Larvae
action->Zecui Larvae.description:
Saving Throw @Check[fortitude|dc:30]

Stage 1 visible lumps as the larvae move but no ill effect (1 day)

Stage 2 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 1} (1 day)

Stage 3 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 2} (1 day)

Stage 4 @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.conditionitems.Item.4D2KBtexWXa6oUMR]{Drained 3} and controlled by the zecui larva (1 day)

Stage 5 the creature dies and an adult @UUID[Compendium.pf2e.pathfinder-monster-core.Actor.vVs0N3mcWYHsyccc]{Zecui} can emerge from the corpse as an Interact action.
